2605.09533.title: Assessment of RAG and Fine-Tuning for Industrial Question-Answering-Applications
2605.09533.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly employed in enterprise question-answering (QA) systems, requiring adaptation to domain-specific knowledge. Among the most prevalent methods for incorporating such knowledge are Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and fine-tuning (FT). Yet, from a cost-accuracy trade-off perspective, it remains unclear which approach best suits industry scenarios. This study examines the impact of RAG and FT on two closed datasets specific to the automotive industry, assessing answer quality and operational costs. We extend the Cost-of-Pass framework proposed by Erol et al. (arXiv:2504.13359) to jointly assess output quality, generation cost, and user interaction cost. Our findings reveal that while premium models perform best out of the box, open-source models can achieve comparable quality when enhanced with RAG. Overall, RAG emerges as the most effective and cost-efficient adaptation method for both closed- and open-source models.
2605.09533.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在企業問答（QA）系統中的應用越來越普遍，這需要對特定領域知識進行調整。<lb/>在納入這些知識的最普遍方法中，檢索增強生成（RAG）和微調（FT）是最常見的。然而，從成本與準確性的權衡角度來看，目前尚不清楚哪種方法最適合行業場景。<lb/>本研究考察了RAG和FT對汽車行業兩個封閉數據集的影響，評估回答質量和運營成本。<lb/>我們擴展了Erol等人提出的通行成本框架（arXiv:2504.13359），以共同評估輸出質量、生成成本和用戶互動成本。<lb/>我們的研究結果顯示，雖然高端模型在開箱即用的情況下表現最佳，但開源模型在使用RAG增強後也能達到可比的質量。<lb/>總體而言，RAG成為了對於封閉和開源模型最有效且成本效益最高的調整方法。
2605.09533.author: Jakob Sturm et.al.
2605.09533.authors: Jakob Sturm, Josef Pichlmeier, Christian Bernhard, Maka Karalashvili, Johannes Klepsch, Georg Groh, Andre Luckow
